channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Georgia Men's Golf Team Finishes Tied for Fifth at Augusta Haskins Award Invitational
item.description: The University of Georgia men's golf team finished tied for fifth at the Augusta Haskins Award Invitational with an impressive 28-under par after the third round. Junior Grayson Wood led the team with...

item.title: Iran seeks permanent peace as Trump issues final warning over Hormuz blockade
item.description: Iran has rejected a new 45‑day ceasefire plan proposed by mediators, insisting instead on a permanent end to the war, as tensions with the United States escalate over the closure of the Strait of Horm...

item.title: UNDP and Tanzanian insurers collaborate to strengthen agricultural insurance
item.description: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in collaboration with the Association of Tanzania Insurers (ATI) has launched a new initiative to strengthen agricultural insurance in Tanzania. This su...

item.title: Ex-Nigerian Army Nurse Accuses Senior Officer of Sexual Harassment and Unlawful Detention
item.description: Matilda Anighoro, wey be former nurse for Nigerian Army Medical Corps, don cry out say she suffer sexual harassment, torture, and detention from her oga dem for work. According to her, wahala start fo...

item.title: Innovative Water Extraction Technology Drives Sustainable Mining Practices
item.description: The article highlights how growing global awareness of water scarcity and sustainability challenges has prompted a shift toward responsible water management in the mining sector. Wheaton Precious Meta...

item.title: Israeli airstrike east of Beirut kills Christian political official amid rising tensions
item.description: An Israeli airstrike on an apartment in Ain Saadeh, a predominantly Christian town east of Beirut, has killed a local official from a Christian political party and two women, escalating internal tensi...

item.title: Eastern Cape police rearrest one of eight detainees who escaped over the weekend
item.description: Eastern Cape authorities successfully rearrested one of eight detainees who escaped from Afsondering police station cells over the weekend. Siphelele Mkhulisi, originally in custody for a murder case,...

item.title: Fresh Violence Hits Jos North As Residents Clash Despite Partial Curfew
item.description: Wahala don burst again for Plateau State as fresh fight jam residents of Anguwan Rukuba in Jos North Local Government Area. The matter start early Wednesday morning, just few hours after government co...

item.title: Trump Rejects Ceasefire Proposal Amid Continued Tensions with Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump has dismissed a ceasefire proposal drafted by intermediary countries as 'not good enough' while Iran also rejected a temporary truce. Tensions between the US and Iran remain ...

item.title: Rescued Bats Released in Ukraine Following Winter Relief Efforts
item.description: Following a particularly harsh winter that significantly impacted Ukraine, wildlife rescuers are finding a heartwarming moment of joy in releasing hundreds of bats back into the wild. These bats were ...

item.title: How Uncanny became a global phenomenon in paranormal entertainment
item.description: The paranormal podcast 'Uncanny' has taken the world by storm since its debut five years ago, captivating millions with spine-chilling ghost stories and intriguing investigations. Hosted by Danny Robi...

item.title: Youth Groups Call for Localised Pipeline Security to Improve Peace and Jobs in Niger Delta
item.description: Plenty youth groups wey dey Rivers State don come together dey urge President Bola Tinubu make e decentralise the pipeline security contracts for Niger Delta. According to the coalition, this move go ...

item.title: Sindh government restricts schools from increasing student transport charges amid fuel price surge
item.description: The Sindh government has formally directed all public and private schools across the province not to raise van fares or student transport fees despite the sharp rise in fuel prices caused by ongoing t...

item.title: MPs Express Concern Over Increasing Number of Mentally Ill State Patients in South African Prisons
item.description: Parliamentary committee members in South Africa have voiced concern over the rising number of declared state patients being held in correctional facilities, which reached 309 by February 2026, up from...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Appoints Dr Ngobani Johnstone Makhubu as New SARS Head
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has named Dr Ngobani Johnstone Makhubu as the new Commissioner of the South African Revenue Service (SARS), effective 1 May 2026, for a five-year term. The appointment, annou...

item.title: Barnsley Murder Investigation: Fifth Arrest Made After Fatal Car Collision
item.description: A fifth person has been arrested on suspicion of murder following a tragic incident in Barnsley, South Yorkshire, where a pedestrian was fatally struck by a car. The victim, identified as 45-year-old ...

item.title: OpenAI Proposes New Economic Framework for AI: Public Wealth Funds, Robot Taxes, and More
item.description: OpenAI has released a set of policy proposals aimed at addressing the economic challenges posed by the rise of superintelligent machines. These proposals include a combination of traditionally left-le...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Results for April 6, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) released the official lotto results for April 6, 2026. Lotto enthusiasts and the general public can check the winning numbers for the different games c...

item.title: Addressing the Police Code of Silence: Key to Tackling Corruption in South Africa’s Law Enforcement
item.description: South African police officers are bound by the law, including the SAPS Code of Conduct and the Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act, to report corruption within their ranks. However, a d...

item.title: Artemis II Mission: Astronauts Set Distance Record on Lunar Flyby
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission is underway, marking the first crewed lunar flyby since the Apollo era. The six-hour flyby, a key preparatory step for future lunar landings near the moon's south pole, has a...
